title: Spergula imbricata
taxonomy: Spergula imbricata is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Caryophyllaceae, commonly known as the pink family. This family consists of approximately 482 genera and 2,046 species. Spergula imbricata is a member of the genus Spergula, which contains around 10 species of annual or biennial plants. The plant is native to Europe, Western Asia, and North Africa, and has been introduced to other regions as a weed. Its classification reflects its evolutionary relationships with other plants in the Caryophyllaceae family.
uses: Spergula imbricata, also known as Lesser Goosefoot or Sticky Mouse-ear, has been traditionally used in folk medicine for its various health benefits. The plant's leaves and stems contain bioactive compounds that may have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. In some cultures, the plant has been used to treat digestive issues, such as diarrhea and stomach ulcers. Additionally, it has been used to reduce fever and alleviate symptoms of colds and flu. More research is needed to confirm the efficacy and safety of using Spergula imbricata for medicinal purposes.

constituents: Spergula imbricata, also known as Lesser Bird's-foot Trefoil, is a plant species that contains various active constituents, including alkaloids, flavonoids, and phenolic acids.
